sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 45-year-old male, presented with an ankle injury sustained during a basketball game with his 10-year-old son.
He landed awkwardly and twisted his ankle, resulting in pain and swelling.
The patient reports exquisite tenderness to palpation on the lateral side of the ankle, with some tenderness on the medial deltoid region.
He describes the ankle as unstable, making it difficult to walk.
The patient is concerned about disrupting his recent progress in working out and getting back to a good physical condition.
He has a history of playing basketball with his son, and his son is a fan of the sport.
The patient also volunteers to coach his son's basketball team starting in April.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The examination revealed swelling on the lateral side of the ankle, with exquisite tenderness to palpation.
The skin is intact, and capillary refill is brisk.
There is no malalignment.
The patient reports pain when standing and walking on the affected ankle.
Test Results: An X-ray of the right ankle showed a displaced lateral malleolus fracture at the Weber C level, with no evidence of medial or posterior malleolar fractures.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral malleolar fracture, which is a displaced fracture on the lateral side of the ankle, resulting in instability and pain.
Problem list: Lateral malleolar fracture; unstable ankle; ankle pain; potential need for surgery.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam for pain management and to reduce swelling.
I instructed the patient to ice the ankle and elevate it regularly.
I recommended that the patient use crutches to avoid putting weight on the affected ankle.
I advised the patient to continue coaching his son's basketball team and to start exercising again within three months, pending a successful surgical outcome.
Follow-up: I will follow up with the patient in three months to assess the healing of the fracture and to discuss the possibility of returning to physical activity.
